focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gerald warns Jack against going north, citing connections and danger, while tension grows between Jack and Anna; Jack insists on investigating despite warnings.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carter decides to investigate his brother's death despite warnings from Gerald Fletcher, defying authority and setting his goal.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to find out what happened
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: committed to northern investigation
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: danger from northern associates and strained loyalty to London bosses
evidence_quotes: GERALD We don't want you to go up the North, Jack.
JACK To find out what happened.
GERALD You work for us, Jack. We have connections in those parts. I'd hate you to screw 'em up.
JACK Too bad.

relation_updates: tension with Gerald escalates into open defiance
status_updates: positioned as outsider within London power structure

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter exits the train with others, pauses to look around, and crosses to a pub opposite on a deserted street.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carter exits the train, pauses to assess his surroundings, and deliberately crosses to a pub opposite the station.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: arriving at destination and initiating next phase of journey
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carter is on foot in Newcastle at night, having left the station and heading toward a pub as his first point of contact.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: purpose of visit and immediate objective remain unclear
evidence_quotes: Carter exits with other passengers from the train.
He pauses and looks about
crossing to the pub opposite.

status_updates: on foot in Newcastle at night
moving toward pub as first point of contact

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 18、INT. FRANK CARTER'S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: In a dark room, a man named Carter visits his deceased brother Frank in a coffin, closes the shroud over his face, and leaves, restoring the darkness.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carter covers his dead brother's face and turns off the light, marking the end of his final visit.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking closure for his brother's death
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: leaves the room in darkness, physically and emotionally isolated
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: grief remains unspoken and internalized
evidence_quotes: Carter stands over the coffin.
He gently touches his brother's crossed hands
and folds the shroud over his face.
He exits, turning out the light and closing the door. Darkness returns.

relation_updates: final physical interaction with deceased brother

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter and Eddie reflect on the unexpected nature of the deceased's actions, expressing disbelief over his sudden, uncharacteristic act.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carter begins to question the circumstances of his brother's death after hearing coworkers express surprise over his uncharacteristic actions.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: beginning to question the official narrative of Frank's death
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carter shifts from passive mourning to noticing inconsistencies in his brother's reported behavior before death.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Why would a man known for calmness commit a sudden, violent act?
evidence_quotes: KEITH I couldn't believe it when I heard.
CARTER What?
KEITH I mean, I was surprised when he didn't turn up for work. He was always on time.
CARTER (quietly) Yeah. A bloody funny thing!

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter, at a pub, tasks Keith to alert him if anyone asks. He seeks Albert Swift, his former school gang leader, believing Albert knows about the town's affairs, and mentions he's staying at the Las Vegas behind the dance hall.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carter takes active steps to gather information by instructing Keith to notify him of any inquiries and identifying Albert Swift as a potential source of local knowledge.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: locate Albert Swift to learn about Frank's circumstances
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: positioned as an outsider seeking information, beginning to build a personal network in the town
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Frank's drinking habits and the true cause of his death
evidence_quotes: Carter puts his hand out to restrain him.
Keith, if anybody comes in here and asks for me, you let me know. Right?
Do you know a man called Albert Swift?
He'll know what's going on in this town.

relation_updates: asserts authority over Keith by restraining him
status_updates: establishes presence at Las Vegas behind the dance hall
reveals ongoing sobriety amid others' drinking

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter investigates his brother's car, found in a scrapyard by the River Tyne. Owner Billy Laws claims it was driven into the river due to drunkenness, with no mechanical faults.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carter finds his brother's car in a scrapyard and challenges the explanation that it was driven into the river due to drunkenness, noting the vehicle shows no mechanical faults.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: determine the true cause of his brother's death
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carter becomes skeptical of the official account and begins personally investigating the circumstances of his brother's death
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The car had no mechanical issues, but the claim that his brother was drunk remains unverified and contested by Carter's doubts
evidence_quotes: CARTER This is my brother's car.
CARTER Was the steering faulty?
CARTER What about the brakes?
CARTER How'd it happen, then?

relation_updates: Carter asserts kinship to his brother through ownership of the car
status_updates: Carter is now investigating physical evidence of the death

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter learns from Thorpe that Brumby owns arcades in Westsea and has a new place on Durham Road in Burnham, then leaves abruptly as Edna demands answers.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: extracting intelligence from a captured associate through physical coercion
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carter uses physical violence to force Thorpe to reveal that Cliff Brumby sent him and to disclose Brumby's location.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: identify who is trying to eliminate him
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carter now knows Cliff Brumby is behind the attempt and has the address of Brumby's new place on Durham Road in Burnham
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Brumby's full connection to Frank's death remains unclear
evidence_quotes: Carter grabs him by the testicles and applies pressure. Thorpe screams.
THORPE (desperate) Brumby!
CARTER Where's he living these days? THORPE He's got a new place at Burnham.
THORPE On the Durham Road. The Pantiles.

relation_updates: Thorpe broken from ally to informant under duress

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Albert reveals Eric and his men forced whisky on Frank after he saw the film; Carter, knowing Albert didn't kill Frank, fatally stabs him in revenge.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: culmination of Carter's investigation into Frank's death
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carter learns that Eric and his men forced whisky down Frank's throat after he saw the incriminating film; though Albert admits he did not kill Frank, Carter fatally stabs him in retaliation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: achieve revenge for Frank's death
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carter has killed Albert in retaliation for Frank's death, knowing Albert was not the one who delivered the fatal blow
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Eric remains at large and unpunished
evidence_quotes: I thought they'd just duff him up a bit. Honest.
CARTER I know you didn't kill him. (and again.) I know you didn't.
Carter drives the knife into him with all his might.

relation_updates: ends coercive relationship with Albert through murder
status_updates: commits first confirmed killing in pursuit of justice
auxiliary.persona_anchor: driven by vengeance, capable of cold-blooded violence

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter forces Eric to drink whisky, taunting him about his brother Frank, then strikes him with a shotgun after Eric chokes and retches, ending with a violent blow to the head.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carter forces Eric to drink whisky as retribution for Frank's death and strikes him in the head with a shotgun after he chokes.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: enact retribution for Frank's death
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eric is incapacitated after being struck with a shotgun
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: risk of legal consequences or retaliation
evidence_quotes: CARTER Drink up, Eric. Drink up. I want you to drink all of that. Do you understand? Drink up. Just like it was with my brother, Frank.
The whisky is pouring all over Eric's face and suit. It's disgusting to watch.
Eric chokes and retches.
Carter raises the butt of the gun and brings it down on Eric's head.

relation_updates: relationship with Eric becomes lethal and final

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Eric's body is carried by coal bucket to the sea, dropped with slag, and submerged. Carter laughs, walks back, and decides to discard his shotgun.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carter laughs while Eric's body is dumped into the sea, then decides to discard his shotgun, marking a shift from active vengeance to disengagement.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: eliminate evidence and conclude vendetta
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carter begins to step away from violence, indicated by his decision to discard the shotgun.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the danger may persist even though the weapon is to be discarded.
evidence_quotes: Carter, laughing, walks beside it, gun riding on his shoulder.
Carter stands for a moment, turns and walks back along the beach.
He stops, looks at the shotgun, and decides to get rid of it.

relation_updates: final severance of ties with Eric through degrading disposal
status_updates: in possession of murder weapon but intends to discard it

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter is shot and falls at the sea's edge as a sniper with a gold 'J' ring fires from the clifftop, linked to Kinnear's conspiracy.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carter is shot by a sniper while discarding his shotgun, marking the moment he is mortally wounded.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: attempting to exit violent cycle
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carter is mortally wounded and falls to the ground
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: conspiracy remains active and unpunished
evidence_quotes: Below, Carter moves slowly by the edge of the sea.
He raises the shotgun to throw it away.
High on the clifftop a rifle and telescopic lens line up on Carter.
The finger tightens on the trigger and pulls it.

relation_updates: becomes victim of Kinnear's hidden network
status_updates: injured and incapacitated by sniper fire

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter is dead on the beach with a wound to his temple and blood trickling from it, as waves lap around his head.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carter is found dead on the beach with a fatal wound to his temple, marking the end of his involvement in the story.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carter's mission ends in death
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: justice remains incomplete
evidence_quotes: Carter lies on the beach.
The waves lap around his head.
A small trickle of blood pours out of a hole in his temple.
He's dead.

status_updates: body exposed to the sea, symbolizing finality

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Carter journeys from London to Newcastle to investigate his brother Frank's suspicious death, transitioning from a grieving relative to a determined investigator. He confronts a web of corruption tied to local crime figures, uncovering hidden relationships and violent truths. After avenging his brother by killing Eric, Carter is ultimately eliminated by the conspiracy’s unseen enforcers, dying on the beach just as he attempts to discard his weapon and leave violence behind.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Cliff Brumby storms through the party, ejects a boy, and demands his daughter Sandra open her locked bedroom door, while Carter enters the house amid the escalating chaos.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Cliff Brumby interrupts his daughter's party, physically ejects a guest, and demands entry to her locked bedroom, asserting control through force and verbal aggression.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to stop the party and confront Sandra for defying his authority
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: the household is in turmoil, Sandra remains locked in her room in defiance, and Brumby's anger escalates
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Sandra's resistance and the breakdown of trust between father and daughter
evidence_quotes: BRUMBY Jesus wept!
BRUMBY I'll murder the little bitch!
BRUMBY ... spewing over my fucking goldfish.
BRUMBY Sandra! Unlock the door, you bitch.

relation_updates: estranged relationship with daughter Sandra
dominant relationship with Mrs Brumby

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter confronts Brumby, admits a mistake, refuses to reveal his sender, and after Brumby attacks, subdues him with blows before leaving calmly.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Brumby attempts to block Carter's exit and physically attacks him, but is quickly overpowered and collapses into a chair, injured and humiliated.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to determine who sent Carter and reassert control in his home
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: physically defeated and diminished in authority after failing to confront Carter
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: ongoing threat from Carter and uncertainty about who is targeting his operations
evidence_quotes: Brumby swings at him.
Carter moves away from the punch, then applies several sharp blows to Brumby's head and neck.
Brumby groans and collapses into the armchair, hurt.

relation_updates: hostile confrontation with Carter
loss of physical dominance

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Brumby offers Carter $5,000 and the name Cyril Kinnear, claiming Kinnear is responsible for a death linked to Carter's brother, but Carter rejects the unsubstantiated accusation.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Brumby offers Carter $5,000 and claims Cyril Kinnear is responsible for Carter's brother's death, attempting to enlist Carter's help against a mutual threat.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to manipulate Carter into targeting Cyril Kinnear
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carter rejects Brumby's offer due to lack of evidence and distrust of his motives
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Brumby remains under pressure from rival operators and unable to act directly without exposure
evidence_quotes: BRUMBY Kinnear. Cyril Kinnear. (pause) Kinnear did it.

relation_updates: instrumental relationship with Carter
accusatory stance toward Cyril Kinnear

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Brumby confronts Carter, who punches him for showing a film to Frank; Brumby claims it was necessary to reach 'them'.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Brumby is punched by Carter for showing the incriminating film to Frank but maintains it was necessary to reach 'them'.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: committed to reaching 'them' by any means
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: physically confronted and isolated, yet firm in his decision
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: tactical conflict with Carter over methods
evidence_quotes: Carter slams his fist hard into his gut.
CARTER You shouldn't have shown the film to Frank.
BRUMBY I had to. It was the only way I could get at them.

relation_updates: adversarial with Carter

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Cliff Brumby emerges as a volatile patriarch and embattled operator who asserts control through force but is repeatedly undermined—first by his daughter's defiance, then by Carter's physical dominance. As his authority erodes, he shifts from enforcer to manipulator, attempting to redirect Carter against rivals with unverified claims. His actions reveal a desperate effort to maintain influence amid personal and professional decline, marked by isolation and unresolved conflict.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter confronts Eric at a racecourse, mocking his life choices and probing about a man named Albert Swift, hinting at unresolved personal and criminal ties.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carter confronts Eric at the racetrack, challenging his claim of being 'straight' and directly questioning him about Albert Swift, exposing tension and a shared criminal past.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Eric attempts to maintain a respectable front and avoid answering questions about his current associations.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eric's composure is unsettled; his denial of knowing Albert Swift is met with skepticism, increasing his vulnerability to exposure.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Eric denies knowing Albert Swift, but Carter's pointed question casts doubt and leaves the issue unresolved and potentially dangerous.
evidence_quotes: ERIC: Oh, I'm straight. (indicating uniform) Respectable.
ERIC: Can't say I do.
Eric swings round, startled.

relation_updates: Carter asserts dominance over Eric through sarcasm and physical intrusion
Eric shows wariness and defensiveness toward Carter
status_updates: Eric's criminal past is implied despite his current chauffeur role
Eric is linked to Albert Swift through Carter's targeted questioning
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Eric presents himself as reformed and respectable but becomes defensive when questioned about his past or connections.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter confronts Eric about not disclosing his employer, dismisses threats involving Cyril, Sid, and Gerald, and leaves, prompting Eric to send Ray after him.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Eric warns Carter that Cyril, Sid, and Gerald disapprove of his investigation and responds to Carter's defiance by sending Ray to follow him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: protect the group's interests and monitor Carter's movements
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eric takes direct action to track Carter after failing to deter him with group loyalty appeals
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carter's continued investigation threatens the group's stability
evidence_quotes: ERIC Cyril didn't like it, either.
ERIC He's thinking Sid and Gerald won't like it much when they hear you've been sticking your nose in.
Carter leaves. Eric signals Ray to follow him.

relation_updates: tension with Carter escalates to active surveillance
alignment with Cyril, Sid, and Gerald reaffirmed
status_updates: assumes operational control by directing Ray
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Eric shifts from verbal defensiveness to taking concrete steps to enforce group boundaries.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter kills Peter on the ferry roof; Con and Eric flee as Eric arranges to sink Carter's car, drowning Glenda despite her screams.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Eric verbally confronts Carter with threats, then orders the deliberate destruction of Carter's car, resulting in Glenda's death by drowning.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: eliminate Carter's leverage and assert dominance
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eric causes the death of Glenda by ordering the car to be pushed into the river, an unintended but direct consequence of his actions.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carter survives the confrontation, and Glenda's death has not yet led to immediate consequences for Eric.
evidence_quotes: ERIC You're finished, Jack. You know that, don't you? I've bloody finished you.
ERIC Carter, your car needs a wash.
Eric signals to the driver in the Land Rover to bump the Sunbeam into the water.
Glenda's screams are lost as the river wraps itself around the sinking car.

relation_updates: hostility toward Carter reaches lethal intensity
operational bond with Con and Peter confirmed, though Peter is killed
status_updates: authorizes and executes a fatal act of sabotage
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Eric shifts from enforcing threats to initiating lethal actions with collateral harm.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: At a lively party with wealthy middle-aged men and young women, Kinnear moves between rooms as Eric Paice informs him that Gerald Fletcher wants to speak on the phone.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Eric relays a phone message from Gerald Fletcher to Kinnear, positioning himself as a conduit in Kinnear's network.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eric is recognized as someone who handles communications for Kinnear during the party.
evidence_quotes: He puts the receiver down
and crosses to Kinnear.
ERIC Gerald Fletcher wants a word with you Cyril.

relation_updates: Eric acts as intermediary for Kinnear
status_updates: Eric is present and functional within the party's social hierarchy
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Eric operates within the group's social structure as a reliable, low-profile operator.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Eric enters as the noise of a wild party continues; Kinnear requests a private word with him.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Kinnear requests a private conversation with Eric, moving their interaction off the record.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eric is drawn from the party environment into a one-on-one, confidential interaction with Kinnear.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The reason for Kinnear's request is not revealed.
evidence_quotes: Eric comes into the room.
ERIC You want something, Cyril?
KINNEAR Yes, Eric. A word with you.

relation_updates: Kinnear seeks private communication with Eric
status_updates: Eric is available for confidential discussion
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Eric is someone Kinnear chooses to speak with privately, suggesting a level of trust or functional reliance.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter chases Eric across the jetty, threatening him with a shotgun, as Eric flees toward the beach under the structure.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Eric flees from Carter upon being spotted, beginning a chase across the jetty after Carter threatens him with a shotgun.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: escape immediate danger
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: fleeing under threat of gunfire, moving toward the beach beneath the jetty
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: avoid capture or harm by Carter
evidence_quotes: Eric turns around abruptly. He sees Carter and disappears up the nearest stairway.
Carter looks back to the coal jetty, just in time to see Eric scamper down to the level below.
CARTER Stay away from the car or I'll blow you apart.
Eric darts under the jetty.

relation_updates: adversarial with Carter
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Eric's confidence collapses as he becomes a target of direct retribution.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Eric runs painfully on a pebble beach past smoking factory chimneys; Carter appears, watches him, then descends and begins running too.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: subject to physical strain and exposure
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Eric runs across a pebble beach with visible difficulty, struggling to continue as Carter appears and begins pursuit.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: continue escape despite physical limits
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: exhausted and visibly deteriorating while fleeing
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: cannot sustain flight much longer
evidence_quotes: Eric runs out on to the beach.
The only sounds are his feet pounding the pebbles and his hurtful panting.
For a moment he watches Eric stumbling along at the far end of the beach.
He climbs down to the beach and starts running.

relation_updates: being hunted by Carter
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Eric's physical deterioration reflects his increasing vulnerability and loss of control.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter chases Eric, who panics, falls, gets up, and runs around a headland, with Carter following.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Eric panics upon seeing Carter gaining, stumbles and falls, but quickly gets up and continues running around a headland.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: evade capture at all costs
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: continues fleeing with reduced momentum, Carter closing in
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: no clear path to safety
evidence_quotes: Carter is gaining on Eric rapidly.
Eric looks round and panics.
He stumbles and falls over.
He gets to his feet quickly and disappears round the headland.

relation_updates: Carter closing in
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Eric's survival instinct overrides dignity, revealing raw vulnerability.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A desolate black beach bordered by cliffs and littered with debris, where coal slag is transported by bucket chain to sea; Eric struggles in the mud as Carter approaches.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Eric is physically trapped in mud on a desolate beach as Carter approaches, ending his escape attempt.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eric is immobilized and unable to evade capture as Carter closes in.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Eric faces imminent confrontation with no means of escape.
evidence_quotes: Eric is half-way along the beach, struggling in the mud.
Carter closes on him.

relation_updates: Carter imminent
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Eric is reduced to a helpless figure, physically overwhelmed and cornered.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carter forces Eric to drink whisky, taunting him about his brother Frank, then strikes him with a shotgun after Eric chokes and retches, ending with a violent blow to the head.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Forced to drink whisky by Carter as punishment for his role in Frank's death, then struck with a shotgun butt in a fatal assault.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: unconscious and mortally injured
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Eric's complicity in Frank's death is confirmed under duress
evidence_quotes: CARTER Drink up, Eric. Drink up. I want you to drink all of that. Do you understand? Drink up. Just like it was with my brother, Frank.
Eric pours the whisky into his mouth. It spills over and runs down his face.
Eric is choking.

relation_updates: relationship with Carter turns violently hostile
Carter holds Eric responsible for Frank's death
status_updates: subjected to psychological and physical torture
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Eric's final moments expose his guilt and the full cost of his past actions.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Eric's body is carried by coal bucket to the sea, dropped with slag, and submerged. Carter laughs, walks back, and decides to discard his shotgun.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Eric's body is transported by coal bucket and dumped into the sea along with coal slag, erasing his physical presence.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: dead and submerged in the sea
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: no acknowledgment or mourning of Eric's death
evidence_quotes: Eric's body drops into the sea.
The coal slag follows in a cloud of black dust.
The waves pass over and everything is gone.
Carter, laughing, walks beside it, gun riding on his shoulder.

relation_updates: Carter severs all ties by disposing of Eric's body
status_updates: body disposed of in degrading manner
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Eric's death is treated as waste disposal, underscoring his complete dehumanization.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Eric Paice begins as a seemingly reformed figure attempting to maintain a respectable front, but his past ties and allegiances quickly pull him into escalating conflict with Carter. He transitions from evading confrontation to actively enforcing the group's interests, culminating in orchestrating lethal retaliation. His role shifts from enforcer to fugitive as Carter pursues him for his complicity in Frank's death, leading to a violent end where he is physically and symbolically erased.
